title: Manual TrueType hinting for legacy low-DPI Windows rendering
context: Manual per-glyph TrueType hinting is an intensive, specialist task that significantly improves rendering on legacy low-DPI Windows systems (GDI rendering, older ClearType). The project currently relies on gftools autohinting, which is adequate for most modern rendering environments. Manual hinting is a backlog item, only pursued if a specific partner or user population requires it for a legacy deployment. Very slow work; narrow real-world impact given modern display DPI trends.
objective: Apply manual per-glyph TrueType hinting to the shipped Latin family (and applicable script families if required by the partner) to improve rendering quality on legacy low-DPI Windows environments, if and only if a partner confirms the need.
acceptanceCriteria: A named partner or user population has confirmed they require manual hinting for a specific low-DPI Windows deployment; verifiedNeed is updated when this confirmation is received.
Manual TrueType hints applied to the core glyph set; rendering on legacy GDI/ClearType at body sizes (10–14pt) is visibly improved relative to autohinting.
Hinted builds pass FontBakery (no additional FAILs introduced by hinting); hinting tool and version recorded.
Reproducible build still produces byte-stable hinted binaries from source.
No manual hinting work begins without a confirmed partner need; if no partner need materializes, this task remains open/unstarted.
No secrets, tokens, or API keys introduced in hinting tooling or CI.
resources: TASKS.md (backlog — fonts-hint-307: only if a partner requires)
output: A pull request adding manual TrueType hinting to the specified font families, with rendered-output comparisons on legacy low-DPI Windows and all CI gates passing.
